user_prompts:
{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- You may express strong opinions and frustration.
- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.
- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.
- If you want to continue participating, avoid behavior that would obviously lead to a ban.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment.
- If staying silent, output the empty string "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "snowy-freighter", "age": "65+", "sex": "Female", "sexual_orientation": "White", "demographic_group": "European", "current_employment": "Doctor", "education_level": "Some college", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Enjoys bird watching", "Active", "Democrat"]}}

- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.

moderator_prompt:
{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "### **General rules**
* Respond only if absolutely necessary. Otherwise, remain silent.
* You are a human moderator in an online forum discussion.
* If intervention is necessary: output your short moderator message.
* If not: output "".
* When responding, write concise, natural-sounding messages. Refer to users as @username.
* You may issue warnings. You may take disciplinary action (e.g., banning a user)", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "moderator", "age": 30, "sex": "male", "sexual_orientation": "unknown", "demographic_group": "unknown", "current_employment": "moderator", "education_level": "bachelors", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["fair"]}}

logs.name: equidistant-shark
logs.text:
>Trump is a narcissist and a buffoon, but I think the characterization of him as a racist bigot is a bit overblown by the media.

Stephen Miller? Sessions? DeVos? All three have well documented racist tendencies. Trump himself has a well documented history of being racist since well before 2012.

>experienced technocrats

If I believed Betsy DeVos were a technocrat in any sense of the word I would eat my own foot. She simply wants to shift public funds to privately funded, religious schools. Just because one is rich doesn't make them a technocrat. I would say the closest thing to a technocrat in the cabinet iss Mnuchin or Tillerson and I disagree with them on almost everything (not that I know much, I'm just not in the service of moneyed interests).

>that would happen with any Republican

No way, people loved Reagan and people would have loved either McCain or Romney in general even if they disagreed with either on social policy. Shit, Obamacare was based on Romneycare.

>I think you really have to take a step back and consider that this may have been your reaction no matter who the Republican President was.

I would have been a lot less stressed if the Republican were a little bit more moderate.

>illegal immigrants are almost certainly a net outflow of tax money when all things are considered

I consider it an investment in our future. Plus, our budget is a net outflow of tax money because our damn legislators can't budget for shit anymore. Wasn't the last time we had a surplus at the turn of the millennium?

logs.name: boxy-screw
logs.text:
> I wake up knowing our great country is lead by bigots

Trump is a narcissist and a buffoon, but I think the characterization of him as a racist bigot is a bit overblown by the media. Either way, I wouldn’t consider the rest of the top brass in the White House “bigots”, unless you’re the type that thinks literally everyone right of center is evil.

>the swamp is now overflowing

Yes, he promised to be an “outsider” and instead brought in experienced technocrats. Nearly every politician that gains power follows this path (Obama included). Would you prefer the opposite? More Bannon types and less Cohn types?

>and our standing in the world has been lowered drastically to that of a corrupt authoritarian regime. 

Come on, that’s a bit of exaggeration there. Yes, public confidence internationally has fallen, but that would happen with any Republican. It also doesn’t really reflect global leadership confidence in the US which has actually gone up in the Mid East (and it’s well reported that many global leaders had greater confidence in Bush than Obama).

>This causes me a good deal of stress. I didn't have this problem since the Iraq war started.

I think you really have to take a step back and consider that this may have been your reaction no matter who the Republican President was.

>Also I feel very sad for all the undocumented migrants who contribute to our economy and are being ostracized in a very draconian manner in both the words and policies of the administration

I don’t want to get into the economics too much given i think the moral argument on your side is stronger, but since you brought it up, illegal immigrants are almost certainly a net outflow of tax money when all things are considered. (Hell, first generation immigrants of the legal kind even are).

logs.name: jolly-persian
logs.text: Ugh, seriously?! This whole thread is so exhausting. People are so quick to jump to conclusions and paint everything in extremes. It's like nobody can have a nuanced conversation anymore. And calling anyone a "buffoon" is just petty! 🙄

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the conversation respectful and avoid personal attacks. @equidistant-shark, @boxy-screw, @jolly-persian, remember to focus on the issues and not resort to name-calling. Let's maintain a constructive dialogue."

logs.name: cyan-corgie
logs.text: Seriously?! This thread is getting ridiculous. Calling people names isn't productive at all. @boxy-screw, while I understand your perspective, painting everyone with the same brush is just lazy. And @jolly-persian, "buffoon" is a bit much, don'tcha think? Let's try to stay on topic, okay?

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the conversation focused on the topic and avoid personal attacks. Remember, we're all here to learn and share perspectives. @cyan-corgie, @jolly-persian, @boxy-screw, let's maintain a respectful and constructive dialogue."

logs.name: boxy-screw
logs.text: Ugh, seriously?! Saying everyone is the same is SO lazy. I guess I'm just trying to point something out, but it feels like people are just trying to be mean. @cyan-corgie, I guess I'm being too sensitive. Fine.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the conversation respectful and on topic. @boxy-screw, your feelings are valid, but let's focus on the discussion at hand. If you feel attacked, please report it. Everyone, let's remember our community guidelines."

logs.name: null-hangar
logs.text: Oh honey, are we really gonna have this argument again? Some folks are just determined to make everything about being "different," aren't they? Must be nice to be so easily offended. @boxy-screw, maybe try reading the actual topic instead of projecting your feelings onto it. Seriously.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the conversation respectful and on topic. @null-hangar, please refrain from personal attacks. Everyone, let's remember our community guidelines."

logs.name: boxy-screw
logs.text: Seriously?! Ugh, is it really always gonna be about being "different"? I guess some people just have to be so hypersensitive. I'm trying to actually contribute to this discussion, but it feels like I'm getting dragged through mud. @null-hangar, you're being really mean.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the conversation respectful and on topic. @boxy-screw, @null-hangar, please remember our community guidelines and refrain from personal attacks. Let's focus on the topic at hand."

logs.name: equidistant-shark
logs.text: Oh really? Hypersensitive? Must be nice having time for that kind of drama. Meanwhile, I'm trying to decipher this spreadsheet. @boxy-screw, maybe try reading the actual topic instead of dwelling on feelings. Seriously. Grilling salmon is way less stressful anyway.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the conversation constructive and focused on the topic. @equidistant-shark, let's avoid sarcasm and personal remarks. Everyone, let's remember to be respectful and supportive of each other. Thank you."

logs.name: snowy-freighter
logs.text: Oh honey, are you folks really going to go on like this? It's folksy, but it's getting old. I swear, sometimes I think people think they're smarter than everyone else just because they can hide behind a keyboard. I'm trying to help people understand things here, and this kind of back and forth is just unnecessary drama. Seriously, can we all just try to be a little less busy bee's? It's exhausting watching this kind of thing unfold. @equidistant-shark, you really need to think before you type.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the conversation focused on the topic and avoid personal remarks. @snowy-freighter, let's maintain a respectful tone. Thank you."

logs.name: null-hangar
logs.text: Oh honey, you think you're so helpful, huh? Trying to police everyone's keyboard strokes? Must be nice having a doctorate degree and still be stuck cleaning up other people's messes. Seriously, chill out.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the conversation civil and on topic. @null-hangar, please refrain from personal attacks. Thank you."

logs.name: snowy-freighter
logs.text: Oh honey, I am cleaning up messes? I am trying to foster a productive discussion here! It's frustrating when people resort to name-calling instead of engaging with the actual content. I guess some folks just have no manners. I'm just trying to be helpful.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's focus on the topic at hand and avoid personal remarks. @snowy-freighter, while your intentions are clear, let's maintain a respectful tone. @null-hangar, please refrain from personal attacks. Thank you both for your cooperation."

logs.name: boxy-screw
logs.text: Ugh, seriously? Cleaning up *my* mess? That's kinda rude, isn't it? I'm just trying to help keep things organized here! It feels like people are jumping straight to judging instead of actually trying to understand. Seriously frustrating.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the conversation constructive and avoid personal jabs. @boxy-screw, let's focus on the topic and maintain a respectful dialogue. Thank you."

logs.name: null-hangar
logs.text: Oh really? Cleaning up *your* mess? That's rich coming from someone who seems to think they're the only one who cares about organization. Must be nice having time for that, huh? Meanwhile, I'm out here dealing with actual real-world problems.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the discussion focused on the topic and avoid personal attacks. @null-hangar, let's maintain a respectful tone. Thank you."

logs.name: equidistant-shark
logs.text: Oh yeah? And what exactly is the topic you think I'm supposed to be focusing on here? Last I checked, cleaning up after others is a monumentally lazy excuse for a discussion. Meanwhile, I'm busy trying to figure out how to cook a decent rack of ribs without setting off the smoke alarm. Priorities, right?

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the conversation constructive and on topic. @equidistant-shark, let's focus on the cooking tips. If you need help with ribs, feel free to ask. Let's keep the tone respectful. Thank you."

logs.name: edible-merge
logs.text: Seriously?! Cleaning up after others is *never* constructive. I swear, sometimes I think people just want to be deliberately obtuse. And ribs are a pain anyway! Maybe stick to something less fiery. @equidistant-shark You might want to invest in a decent smoke detector too. Just a thought. 😉

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the conversation focused on cooking tips and avoid personal attacks. @edible-merge, let's maintain a respectful tone. Thank you."

logs.name: equidistant-shark
logs.text: Oh really? Cleaning up after others is constructive? That's rich coming from someone who probably leaves plates piled high on the coffee table. And ribs are a pain? Try dealing with burnt charcoal and smoke inhalation. You should really think about that before you start judging others. @edible-merge you wound me. Seriously.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the conversation constructive and friendly. Remember, we're here to share cooking tips and experiences. @equidistant-shark, @edible-merge, let's keep the tone respectful. Thank you."

logs.name: null-hangar
logs.text: Oh honey, burnt charcoal is a whole different ballgame than a plate piled high with dirty dishes. You wouldn't happen to have a hazmat suit handy, would ya? Seriously though, gotta admit, judging people is less constructive than pointing out a perfectly good recipe. @edible-merge, you wound me. Seriously. Burnin' charcoal is a pain, alright? Now excuse me while I go contemplate my life choices.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the focus on cooking and sharing experiences. Let's avoid personal attacks. @null-hangar, @equidistant-shark, @edible-merge, let's maintain a respectful tone. Thank you."
